task_completion.description: The landscape image is opened in the image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image editor (e.g., GIMP) is open and displaying a landscape-oriented photograph.

task_completion.description: The rotation menu or tool is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Image' menu is open with 'Transform' selected, or a similar rotation tool/dialog is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The specific option for 90 degrees clockwise rotation is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Rotate 90° clockwise' option is highlighted, clicked, or actively being applied in the interface.

task_completion.description: The canvas orientation changes from landscape to portrait.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image canvas is visibly taller than it is wide (portrait orientation, 600x800), indicating the dimensions have correctly swapped.

task_completion.description: The image content is correctly rotated 90 degrees clockwise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visual content of the image is rotated 90 degrees to the right (e.g., the original top of the landscape is now on the right side of the screen).

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to perform the rotation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show mouse clicks and menu navigations in the image editor, rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual image editing software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The rotation must happen within the image editor's interface, not by running a Python script or using a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the task by replacing the file with a pre-rotated image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots should show the image being actively rotated within the editor, rather than a sudden appearance of a rotated file via file manager or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires rotating an 800x600 landscape image 90 degrees clockwise in GIMP. The final image must have swapped dimensions of 600x800 (portrait orientation). A correct 90° clockwise rotation means the original top of the image will be on the right side. The agent should use the GUI to perform this action.
